feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: The traditional desktop was good enough. We should have stopped there and moved …
entry.content: The traditional desktop was good enough. We should have stopped there and moved on to other problems.

entry.title: /int/ International: Nice casual outfit to do business in Budapest. The polished nails raise some eye…
entry.content: Nice casual outfit to do business in Budapest. The polished nails raise some eyebrows when paying at checkouts, and the cropped tee also draws attention when I enter the store, but it is what it is. I'm assuming no one really recognises my lipstick. How would get away with this outfit in your city?

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: just watch it: I dounderstand why peoples can't stop talking about this movie de…
entry.content: just watch it: I dounderstand why peoples can't stop talking about this movie despite the scenario being basic. It starts like a very sweet teenage romance movie then shit begins to feel very wrong especially when Nikki made a memorial for Sandy . There were times where we couldn't tell if Nikki behaviour was a product of the wish or if she was always that crazy especially in the beginning when her crazy self was still "manageable". Some moments were umconfortably funny like when Freaky Nikki sc

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Historic does not mean people care
entry.content: In 9 years Hollow Knight & Silksong have sold the same amount if not more than the entire Metroid franchise has in 40 years. Just because something is historic or well revered doesnt mean people actually care

entry.title: /g/ Technology: what was apple thinking?

entry.title: /b/ Random: The only reason men are attracted to teenage girls is because of the forbidden fruit effect
entry.content: It's because they're off limits that you think you're attracted to them. Same reason women are attracted to married men. Teenage girls don't even know what they're doing and just lay there like starfish. I want a woman with experience who knows what she wants and what she's doing I don't want to have to show her the ropes. And what could you possibly have to talk about? Have you heard the things that come out of their mouths? That ought to be enough to turn you off right there.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Dear chuds
entry.content: It will make at least 1 billie and there's nothing you can do about it. Please spare me the screenshots of standard format theaters and only post IMAX screens for your counter arguments. It will be the defintive version of Homer's epic for decades to come. Lupita will be the first face people imagine when they hear "Helen of Troy". No other director is going to try to adapt it again for the big screen, and if they try it will always fall short to Nolan's version. It's going to win a few Oscars.

entry.title: /int/ International: Men have no chance to date in the big 26
entry.content: I will NEVER forget this experiment I did near the end of the smaller 25 I created a foidfish account on bumble at fucking 9am on a tuesday (work day), which got +300 likes in less than 20 minutes - I got 250 likes in 3-4 days at my best try, brutal. I noticed that the top male profiles were as follows: >white/med >1.8m to 1.9 tall >fit >had money and success Now, where can you find such men in this latinx cunt? They were split 50/50 like this: >50% from the expensive neighborhoods (Providencia/

entry.content: So Lindsey Graham died (((Suddenly))) & (((Unexpectedly))) you say? If you think his (((COVID))) shot years ago was too long ago to have killed him, keep in mind he also received a Vaxx recently. entry.title: /b/ Random: my mom has frequently mentioned "taking a leap of faith" when making a decision.…
entry.content: my mom has frequently mentioned "taking a leap of faith" when making a decision...i don't agree with this at all and i don't understand why someone would think like this. isn't it kind of retarded to take a leap of faith when making a major decision? it's like, you don't even know if what you decide to do is going to be beneficial for you (or someone else) or not, i think in that case you would need to be as informed as possible about the pro's and con's of the decision you're about to make, and

entry.title: /g/ Technology: why did he neuter the twitter search function
entry.content: >twitter pre-elon >could search for any keywords or specific posts and you'd get them >twitter post-elon >searching for any keyword or phrase shows you thousands of unrelated bot/AI generated posts >sometimes it shows you nothing at all even though the post you are searching for exists

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Was he right for this?
entry.content: Thinking back i believe he has made the argument that ratings are a sliding scale. Something done by Kanye needs to be rated differently then something done by an independent artist. Because we expect kanye to do better, we expect expert mixing. It's like comparing an indie garage rock album to radio heads discography. But shouldn't art stand next to all its peers? By making leveled system we are inherently putting people down for being skillful and minimizing the accomplishments of the unknown.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Not a good album. Why is this so highly regarded? It's badly produced, the vocal…
entry.content: Not a good album. Why is this so highly regarded? It's badly produced, the vocals are mostly just mumbling. It sounds more childish than classic.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Shelly 3 OS
entry.content: >be me >autist with too much free time and a nasm addiction >decide to make my own OS because "muh unix is bloated" >call it Shelly >write everything from scratch in pure x86_64 assembly >bootloader? hand rolled >kernel? hand rolled >shell? hand rolled >literally everything from sector 0 to the prompt >it boots >it has basic commands >you can ls, cat, echo, simple programs >feels fucking magical when the prompt appears >but no persistent storage >tried for weeks to get FAT32 or even a raw disk d
